title: S2/G5: Pin down the SHARP connectivity function f_O(n,H) and the correct range of H

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Statement-precision gap (off-by-one / quantifier).** The characterization is only correct with the *sharp* connectivity function and the *correct* set of subgroups quantified over.

**To resolve.**
- Give the explicit closed form of $f_{\aO}(n,H)$ (e.g. $\lceil n/|H|\rceil$ in the regular/complete case) and justify it from the generator computation (G3).
- Decide precisely which $H$ the 'for every $H$' ranges over: all subgroups, or only those reachable/relevant in the transfer system $\aO$. A wrong range makes the theorem either false or vacuous (quantifier error F4).
- Check floor-vs-ceiling and $\rho_H$ vs reduced regular representation $\bar\rho_H$ conventions; a single off-by-one breaks both directions.

Reference: Hill primer (slice connectivity vs Postnikov connectivity comparison).
